title: Paths, Navigation, and Filesystem Hierarchy · Command Brief: Path Expansion
summary: Locate application configuration and logs on a workstation whose current directory and distribution conventions differ from a familiar machine. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux path expansion increment in a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish tilde, parameter, command, arithmetic, pathname, and quote-removal expansion before command execution.
Relate root-level directories to boot, configuration, devices, variable data, user data, runtime state, and optional software roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Path Expansion

content.text: Distinguish tilde, parameter, command, arithmetic, pathname, and quote-removal expansion before command execution. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: On a worked example host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. A defensible solution applies Path Expansion, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook.

content.code:
pwd
printf 'shell=%s\n' "$BASH_VERSION"
type -a printf
command -V pwd
printf 'status=%d\n' "$?"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
